name: deckit
description: An end-to-end image-first deck workflow with a recommendation gate, PPTX/PDF final delivery, and an automatic vertical preview image.

interface.shortDescription: v0.4.0 - Create image-first slide decks with generated full-slide images and PPTX/PDF delivery.
interface.longDescription: Version: 0.4.0. Deckit coordinates an end-to-end image-first deck production workflow: ingest text, PDF, URL, or a topic seed; recommend a production plan before expensive generation unless defaults are requested; shape the argument; plan slide storyboards; write image-generation prompts; invoke the official $imagegen skill to produce full-slide images; package those images into the requested final delivery target; produce a standard medium-size vertical preview image; and run review/audit before delivery. Supported final delivery targets are non-editable image-container PPTX and image-only PDF. Artifacts are written under the user's active working directory, preferably outputs/<run-name>, and Deckit should not leave mirrored production runs in the plugin installation directory. Generated slide PNGs are retained as intermediate artifacts, and dist/preview.png is produced automatically for quick visual inspection and mobile-friendly scanning. When explicitly invoked, Deckit treats topic-only requests such as "summarize this article" or "explain this research topic" as deck-production requests, not plain prose answers and not prompt-pack-only runs. It first offers A/B/C choices to accept the recommendation, expand options, or add custom requirements, then continues without additional stage-by-stage confirmation. Ambiguous requests such as "make a PPT" or "create a presentation" are handled as image-first slide generation. Deckit does not create editable native PowerPoint decks; a .pptx is only a non-editable container for generated full-slide images and must audit as exactly one full-slide picture per slide with no native editable slide content.
defaultPrompt: Turn this transcript into a complete image-first deck with generated full-slide images, PPTX packaging, and a preview image.
Explain this research topic as an image-first deck.
Make a complete image-first deck; first recommend a plan and ask whether to accept it, expand options, or add custom requirements.
